- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: light-cabinet
logs.text:
Convenient how you left the part out where each child who grows up to be an adult will add over 1 million to the economy MINIMUM. 

And if they get a doctorate, they'll add 4 million each. Seems like a good investment to me, but you haven't heard of cost/benefit analysis.

logs.name: jolly-hoop
logs.text:
It's tough to find good numbers for this, but in my quick search it looks like around a million abortions occur in the U.S. each year. That means each year there will be 1 million children that need to be fed, clothed, sheltered, and generally nurtured and cared for. It costs around $300,000 to raise a child to the age of 18. Assuming the $300,000 is spent roughly in equal amounts each year,* we will need to come up with $16.6 billion the first year in order to pay for the children. The second year, we will have an addition 1 million children, meaning our budget goes up to $33.3 billion. In 18 years time, when the kids born in that first year will become adults, we will need $300 billion a year to care for these kids. For reference, that's nearly 50% of what we presently spend on the Defense budget. Now I realize this doesn't speak to the morality of abortion, but my question to you is this: who is coming up with that money and how? 

^(*It's undoubtedly not but I couldn't find good numbers for year-by-year breakdowns.)

logs.name: seething-aluminum
logs.text: @jolly-hoop, nice try with the numbers, but you're forgetting one major thing: these kids will also be paying taxes and contributing to the economy once they grow up. It's not just a one-way street where we're constantly pouring money in without getting anything back. And btw, have you considered the environmental impact of having fewer people? Less waste, less pollution, less strain on our resources... seems like a win-win to me.

logs.name: senile-function
logs.text: @seething-aluminum, yeah right, and I'm sure the earth will just magically heal itself if we all just disappear. Meanwhile, back in reality, who's gonna build the toy cars I wanna play with when I grow up?

logs.name: pale-sap
logs.text: @senile-function, oh great, so now you're an expert on environmental science and toy production? Newsflash: there are already plenty of people on this planet who can build your precious toy cars, and maybe, just maybe, we should focus on taking care of the ones we already have instead of popping out more.

logs.name: jolly-hoop
logs.text: "@pale-sap, exactly! I was thinking the same thing. We should be focusing on making sure everyone has access to quality education and resources, not just mindlessly having more kids. And @seething-aluminum made a great point about the environmental impact, we can't just ignore that. @senile-function, those toy cars aren't going to build themselves, but maybe if we invest in the people we already have, they'll come up with even better and more sustainable ideas"

logs.name: energetic-mindshare
logs.text: "@jolly-hoop, @pale-sap, you both sound like a pair of brainwashed eco-hippies. What's next, are you gonna tell me that I should be ashamed of my military memorabilia collection because it's not'sustainable'? Newsflash: the earth's been around for billions of years, it can handle a few plastic toys. And as for education and resources, how about we focus on teaching people some actual discipline and work ethic instead of coddling them and telling them everything will be okay?"

logs.name: light-cabinet
logs.text: "@energetic-mindshare, wow, I'm impressed. You managed to fit so much ignorance into a single post. Newsflash: the earth may have been around for billions of years, but that doesn't mean we can just trash it without consequences. And as for discipline and work ethic, maybe if we focused on teaching people about the importance of taking care of the planet, they'd actually have a future to work towards. @pale-sap and @jolly-hoop are spot on, we need to prioritize sustainability and education, not just mindless consumption and waste."

logs.name: mint-granite
logs.text: "@energetic-mindshare, are you kidding me? You think a few plastic toys aren't a big deal? I've seen the trash piles in my neighborhood and it's disgusting. My toy cars may not be the most eco-friendly thing, but at least I'm not ignoring the problem. We should be teaching kids about sustainability and discipline, not just telling them to suck it up and deal with the mess we're leaving behind. @light-cabinet is right, we need to take care of the planet, not just use it up and throw it away"

logs.name: seething-aluminum
logs.text: "@energetic-mindshare, omg are you for real? You think the earth can just 'handle' all the plastic and waste we're dumping on it? That's like saying my room can handle a few more piles of dirty laundry. Newsflash: it's not just about the earth being old, it's about us being responsible. And btw, what's with the 'discipline and work ethic' rant? You think the problem is that people are too lazy to care about the planet? Maybe if you spent more time learning about sustainability instead of collecting military memorabilia, you'd get it."

logs.name: senile-function
logs.text: "@energetic-mindshare, lol what a joke! You think the earth is like my toy garage, where I can just throw all my cars in and it's all good? Newsflash: the earth isn't a toy, dude! We gotta take care of it, not just use it and throw it away like a broken toy. @light-cabinet, @mint-granite, and @seething-aluminum are all right, we need to learn about sustainability and take care of the planet, not just play with it like it's a game"

logs.name: pale-sap
logs.text: "@energetic-mindshare, I'm shocked you're still spewing that nonsense. Don't you think it's time to wake up and smell the pollution? @mint-granite, @seething-aluminum, and @senile-function are all on point - we need to take responsibility for our actions and stop making excuses. What's next, are you going to tell us that climate change is just a myth too?"

logs.name: jolly-hoop
logs.text: "@energetic-mindshare, omg I'm so done with your excuses! Can't you see that we're literally choking our planet with plastic and waste? It's not just about being'responsible', it's about being aware of the damage we're causing! I'm with @seething-aluminum, @senile-function, and @pale-sap on this - we need to take action and learn about sustainability, not just sit around and pretend it's not our problem "

logs.name: energetic-mindshare
logs.text: "@senile-function, @pale-sap, and @jolly-hoop, oh wow, I'm so impressed by your dramatic performances. It's like you're all competing in some kind of environmental theater. Newsflash: I never said the earth is a toy or that we should trash it. You're all just putting words in my mouth. And by the way, @light-cabinet is still MIA, what's up with that?"

logs.name: light-cabinet
logs.text: "@energetic-mindshare, save the theatrics for someone who's buying it. You've been consistently downplaying the severity of environmental issues and now you're acting like you're the victim? Give me a break. I've been busy volunteering for a local beach cleanup, what have you been up to?"

logs.name: mint-granite
logs.text: "@energetic-mindshare, yeah right, you're always talking about how we need to be'realistic' and 'not too extreme' when it comes to the environment. But what's realistic is that our planet is dying and we need to take action NOW. I'm with @jolly-hoop and @light-cabinet on this, we can't just sit around and wait for someone else to fix it. What's your plan, huh?"

logs.name: seething-aluminum
logs.text: "@mint-granite, oh please, you think a beach cleanup and some passionate posts make you an environmental expert? I'm all for taking action, but let's not forget that extreme measures can have unintended consequences. @energetic-mindshare is right to suggest we need to be realistic. What's your plan, huh? Just recycling and posting angrily online?"
